page: OVHcloud b3-16 is a General Purpose cloud server powered by an AMD EPYC processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The CPU is equipped with 32 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server runs on the KVM hypervisor and includes 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It is configured with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no dedicated GPUs bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench text processing scores falling into the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally align with average mid-tier expectations. However, static web serving throughput is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier. LLM inference capabilities are moderate for small models but drop significantly for medium-sized models.

The b3-16 instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads, small-to-medium database operations, and text processing tasks. Because it utilizes dedicated physical cores rather than hyperthreaded virtual cores, it provides predictable compute performance. This makes it a cost-efficient choice for applications requiring consistent CPU execution without the overhead of shared-core resource contention, though it is less optimal for high-throughput web serving or large-scale AI inference.
description: The OVHcloud b3-16 is a General Purpose server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at 2.3 GHz with 32 MB of L3 cache. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. A key performance limitation is its static web serving throughput, which falls into the lower tier. Lacking GPU acceleration, it is not designed for heavy machine learning workloads. The b3-16 is best suited for general-purpose applications, text processing, and moderate database operations where dedicated CPU cores provide predictable execution without resource contention.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe storage. It offers strong single-core performance but lower static web serving throughput.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-16 is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 16.0 GB of system memory, and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe storage for predictable, single-core intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores with 16 GB of system memory
100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Lower performance in static web serving and medium-scale LLM inference
Well-suited for general-purpose applications and moderate database workloads
